content: Just as human language learners use lexical reflection (the linguistic sense of reflexion) to deepen mastery of new vocabulary by connecting new terms to prior contextual understanding, we can reframe LLM self-refinement as a form of linguistic reflexion for large language models, where models iteratively map their own imperfect outputs to implicit norms of coherent, correct generation the same way students reflect on word usage to refine their command of a language.
